trans-unit.source: Manipulating the Attribute Table
trans-unit.target: Manipulating the Attribute Table

trans-unit.source: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__
trans-unit.target: __🔙[Back to Homepage](/content/intro.md)__

trans-unit.source: Add field
trans-unit.target: Add field

trans-unit.source: Add a field to the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Add a field to the attribute table.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Add a field for population density, data type: Float or Double or Real (floating point numbers) :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Add a field for population density, data type: Float or Double or Real (floating point numbers) :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Delete field
trans-unit.target: Delete field

trans-unit.source: Delete a field from the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Delete a field from the attribute table.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Delete all unused/unnecessary fields from the layer. :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Delete all unused/unnecessary fields from the layer. :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Calculate Field
trans-unit.target: Calculate Field

trans-unit.source: Calculate the attribute values for a field, e.g. based on the values of other fields.
trans-unit.target: Calculate the attribute values for a field, e.g. based on the values of other fields.

trans-unit.source: In QGIS you can create a new field or update an existing field.
trans-unit.target: In QGIS you can create a new field or update an existing field.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Calculate the population density using the already existing fields Population and Area. :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Calculate the population density using the already existing fields Population and Area. :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Basic Statistics for Fields
trans-unit.target: Basic Statistics for Fields

trans-unit.source: Generates a statistic for a specific field in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Generates a statistic for a specific field in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: Statistics for the field population density for all countries, what is the maximum value, average, etc.? :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: Statistics for the field population density for all countries, what is the maximum value, average, etc.? :open:
:::

trans-unit.source: Statistics by Categories
trans-unit.target: Statistics by Categories

trans-unit.source: Create aggregated statistics for categories.
trans-unit.target: Create aggregated statistics for categories.

trans-unit.source: For which fields in the attribute table should statistics be calculated?
trans-unit.target: For which fields in the attribute table should statistics be calculated?

trans-unit.source: Which field in the attribute table contains the category?
trans-unit.target: Which field in the attribute table contains the category?

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Example: How many cities with more than 300,000 inhabitants are there per country? For each country: How many people live in the largest agglomeration? :open:
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Example: How many cities with more than 300,000 inhabitants are there per country? For each country: How many people live in the largest agglomeration? :open:
:::
